description_long: This object is a flintlock-style handgun with a robust, dark wooden grip and a short, thick metal barrel. The grip is smoothly contoured and shows signs of wear, with a rich brown coloration and a slightly glossy finish. The barrel is cylindrical, metallic, and dark, with a single metal band securing it to the wooden frame. The trigger guard is thin and metallic, curving gently beneath the trigger, while the hammer and flint mechanism are visible above the grip. There are no visible modern features such as a magazine or slide markings, and the overall design is reminiscent of early 19th-century pistols. The surface of the metal components appears slightly aged, with a matte to semi-gloss finish and some patina.
description: A vintage flintlock handgun with a dark wooden grip and a short, thick metal barrel, featuring a classic curved trigger guard and aged metal components.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the wooden grip, metal barrel, and flintlock mechanism.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular barrel opening and the alignment of the barrel with the grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the smooth wooden grip and the metal band securing the barrel.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the grip and the top of the hammer mechanism.
attribute_grip_design: The grip is smoothly contoured with a rounded, ergonomic shape that fits comfortably in the hand. It is made of dark, polished wood with a subtle sheen, showing natural wood grain and some wear from handling. The surface is smooth without visible checkering or stippling, and there are no finger grooves or logos present. The grip curves gently downward, providing a classic, vintage appearance typical of flintlock pistols.
attribute_trigger_guard_style: The trigger guard is thin, metallic, and gently curved, forming a simple oval loop beneath the trigger. It lacks any undercuts, squared-off edges, or integrated accessories, and there is no visible texturing or reinforcement. The design is minimalist and functional, consistent with early handgun styles.
attribute_barrel_type: The barrel is short, thick, and cylindrical with a smooth exterior profile. It is not threaded, ported, or fluted, and there are no compensators or suppressor threads visible. The finish is dark and matte, with a single metal band securing it to the frame and no visible markings or embellishments.
attribute_slide_markings: There are no visible markings, engravings, or inscriptions on the barrel or frame. The surface is plain, with no manufacturer logos, model names, serial numbers, or decorative patterns apparent.
attribute_magazine_base_shape: There is no visible magazine base or floorplate, as this is a flintlock-style handgun that does not utilize a detachable magazine. The grip terminates in a rounded, smooth wooden end without any extensions, bevels, flares, finger rests, logos, or texturing.
